issues.title: Creating multiple Triggers for the same Broker results in Deployments fighting over the same pods.

labels.name: lifecycle/stale
labels.description: Denotes an issue or PR has remained open with no activity and has become stale.

labels.name: kind/bug
labels.description: Categorizes issue or PR as related to a bug.

issues.body:
**Describe the bug**


The [code below](https://github.com/knative-extensions/eventing-rabbitmq/blob/519852a36f044dec7d79421320b6d56d26d03400/pkg/reconciler/trigger/resources/dispatcher.go#L168) in creates Labels which contain only the broker name, thus multiple Triggers will create Deployments with non unique Selector Labels.

```go
func DispatcherLabels(brokerName string) map[string]string {
	return map[string]string{
		eventing.BrokerLabelKey:           brokerName,
		"eventing.knative.dev/brokerRole": "dispatcher",
	}
}
```


 **Expected behavior**
I expected the Deployments to have unique selectors so they only manage their own Replicas/Pods.


 **To Reproduce**
Create one Broker and two Trigger all in the same namespace.

 **Knative release version**
Currently all versions as far as I can tell

 **Additional context**
Add any other context about the problem here such as proposed priority

issues.title: Broker ingress closes connection to rabbitmq to early

labels.name: kind/bug
labels.description: Categorizes issue or PR as related to a bug.

issues.body: **Describe the bug** We have seen that the broker ingress will stop the rabbitmq connections but is still accepting HTTP requests. This ends in a lot of errors during this shutdown phase. **Expected behavior** The ingress stops first accepting HTTP requests (readiness probe?) and stops the rabbitmq connections at the very end of the shutdown **To Reproduce** Send cloudevents using http to the ingress and then trigger a restart of the ingress **Knative release version** 1.20.x **Additional context**

issues.title: Allow specifying replica count of rabbitmq broker-ingress
issues.body:
**Problem**
As far as I can tell, when creating a broker with RabbitmqBrokerConfig, there is no way to specify the replica count of the ingress-broker deployment.
This means that if my autoscaling decides to move the pod to a different node, I cannot send cloudevents during that time.
This can easily be solved by adding a parameter to the broker type or to the configuration.
As far as I can tell from looking a bit at the code, there is no reason multiple pods cannot act together (as all they do is send messages to the rabbit).

As far as I can tell, none of the brokers today have any option for scaling the ingress.

**Exit Criteria**
The ability to set number of replicas in the deployment generated by the Broker

**Additional context (optional)**
I might be missing some configuration option, for now I am manually updating the deployment count of the ingress and hoping that it won't get restored when I'm not looking

issues.title: RabbitMQ channels
issues.body:
**Problem**
To get knative eventing running in a good state using a RabbitMQ Broker its necessary to have RabbitMQChannels implemented so RabbitMQ can also be used in Flows (sequence and parallel). 

At the moment only InMemoryChannel seems to be available for Flows (or deploy Kafka or so).

**[Persona:](https://github.com/knative/eventing/blob/main/docs/personas.md)**
Which persona is this feature for?
maybe `System Integrator`?

**Exit Criteria**
RabbitMQ Channels are available

**Time Estimate (optional):**
How many developer-days do you think this may take to resolve?

**Additional context (optional)**
It looks like there was already work done here but it looks it got "canceled" or at least its not finished yet.
https://github.com/knative-extensions/eventing-rabbitmq/issues/241
https://github.com/knative-extensions/eventing-rabbitmq/pull/358
https://github.com/knative-extensions/eventing-rabbitmq/issues/396

issues.title: CrossNamespaceEventLinks not supported

labels.name: kind/bug
labels.description: Categorizes issue or PR as related to a bug.

issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
I want to use cross namespace event link with the RabbitMQ broker but it looks like it is not implement. 
It also does not check if cross namespace is enabled and thus the errors are not really useful.

I guess the best place where we can see that its not working is here:
https://github.com/knative-extensions/eventing-rabbitmq/blob/main/pkg/reconciler/trigger/controller.go#L160

 **Expected behavior**
- Upstream features are supported
or
- If upstream feature is not supported a proper error message is shown

 **To Reproduce**

- Create a RabbitMQ broker in `knative-system`
- Create a tigger in `default` like this
```
apiVersion: eventing.knative.dev/v1
kind: Trigger
metadata:
  name: event-display-trigger
spec:
  brokerRef:
    apiVersion: eventing.knative.dev/v1
    kind: Broker
    name: rabbitmq
    namespace: knative-system
  filter:
    attributes:
      type: dev.knative.sources.ping
  subscriber:
    ref:
      apiVersion: serving.knative.dev/v1
      kind: Service
      name: event-display
```
- see the following error `Failed to lookup Broker for Trigger{error 26 0  broker.eventing.knative.dev "" not found}`

 **Knative release version**
1.17.3

 **Additional context**
Add any other context about the problem here such as proposed priority

issues.title: Deliver message properties to the sink

labels.name: lifecycle/stale
labels.description: Denotes an issue or PR has remained open with no activity and has become stale.

issues.body:
**Problem**
I have a RabbitMQ source configured to send events to a knative service. When I send a message to the queue, an event arrives to my service, but it doesn't have any of the properties (such as "headers" or "reply-to"). In particular, this makes it impossible for me to respond to the message, but even if responding can be handled some other way, I'd still like to be able to receive the headers.

**[Persona:](https://github.com/knative/eventing/blob/main/docs/personas.md)**
Event consumer

**Exit Criteria**
All properties of the message are added to the cloud event delivered to the source's sink

**Time Estimate (optional):**
How many developer-days do you think this may take to resolve?

**Additional context (optional)**
Add any other context about the feature request here.

pullRequests.title: Bring your own broker
pullRequests.body:
<!-- Thanks for sending a pull request! -->

/hold
Hold until I get tests for bring your own broker.

Split the reconciler into two pieces so that we can support bring your own broker without requiring that we install messaging-topology-operator & RabbitMQCluster operator since we can't use them with 'bring your own broker', and it seems silly to require them for that use case.

<!-- 
Describe your changes here- ideally you can get that description straight from
your descriptive commit message(s)! 

- :gift: Add new feature
- :bug: Fix bug
- :broom: Update or clean up current behavior
- :wastebasket: Remove feature or internal logic
-->

- :gift: Provide two versions of the controller, one that does not depend on rabbitmq cluster-operator nor messaging-topology-operator.
- :gift: One supports bring your own rabbitmq cluster
- :bug: If you use the cluster-operator / m-t-o version, we do not put finalizers on the brokers/triggers, which can lead to undeletable resources in some corner cases.

pullRequests.title: rework the ingress of broker for rabbit
pullRequests.body:
Found several issues looking at the implementation of the ingress, notably it was not rejecting non-POST methods, and a few other various return code control things we are suppose to do for passing conformance on Broker specs.

This pulls in the http listener that eventing MTChannelBasedBroker classed brokers use and then pushes the event out onto the rabbit channel like it did before.

I also increased the polling timeout, I found the default 2 minutes is on the edge of flakey for RabbitMQ on azure. The PVC seems to be pretty slow on azure, and also could be a source of issue for the flakes we are seeing. Bumped to 5 minutes.

pullRequests.body:
Alternate version of #201 

See https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UKyjA0nfNyvDIiF86YEeskV5_pZ_kP07AoDHrDUq8Xo/edit#heading=h.los3tdhhh2fb for the general template, though I took a few liberties as I was actually writing it up.

# Changes

Adds end-user documentation for the RabbitMQ Source.

- 📖  End user documentation
- 📖  Moved contributor documentation to `DEVELOPMENT.md`

/kind documentation

**Release Note**

<!--
:page_facing_up: If this change has user-visible impact, write a release note in the block
below. Include the string "action required" if additional action is required of
users switching to the new release, for example in case of a breaking change.

Write as if you are speaking to users, not other Knative contributors. If this
change has no user-visible impact, no release-note is needed.
-->
```release-note
Added user-facing documentation for the RabbitMQ source
```

**Docs**

<!--
:book: If this change has user-visible impact, link to an issue or PR in
https://github.com/knative/docs.

See here for guidance on getting permanent links to files: https://help.github.com/en/articles/getting-permanent-links-to-files

Please use the following format for linking documentation:
- [knative/docs]: <issue or pr link>
- [Feature Track]: <link>
- [Usage]: <link>
- [Other doc]: <link>
-->
```docs
Will update the sources docs to point to this page once merged.
```
